feedback.text: Greetings European Commission! I am wirting on the behalf of our current situation on how digital entertainment are being preserved as of modern times and to today. In the early days we had few options on how we consumed digital entertainment and other media such as video games and movies at home. The options were strictly physical. We bought and owned the media we consumed. As the internet came by and breakthrougs pushed through, media became gradually more accessible via the internet. Services became accessible and some would then be needed to be accessed through servers which were provided by the companies that owned the media such as Massive Multiplayer Online games such as "RuneScape" and "World of Warcraft". Even though you bought "World of Warcraft", one day that game may no longer be available due to end of service such as games as "The Crew" by Ubisoft. Back to "World of Warcraft" which is a live service game may change multiple times throughout its lifecycle. Before the launch of "Word of Warcraft: Classic" which is a legacy version of the game, people who then wished to access that current version of the game had to access this version through private services provided by the community. Due to the high demand of one service which were a so called private server, the company who owned "World of Warcraft" made its move and shut down the service due to the intellectual property ownership. It is understandable that Blizzard (the owner) took down the service, but what is not understandable is how they are not designing their games to not be accessed anymore if that current version had been updated to a state which is hugely different from what the version you wished to access and no longer available. I may have singeled out "Massive Multiplayer Online" games MMOs. But I do think that people should have a chance to be able to access any type of multiplayer games and host the games if no servers/ services are available anymore. And therefore I do think developers/ publishers should take in mind such neccesities. The topic which I discussed above may be one of the harder problems to tackle. But if we look to games which were needed to be validated through a server which no longer are available. This is a much easier problem to be tackeled. "Heroes Might and Magic 4" is a game that you may encounter problems with due to such problems. You may play the original game but never access the downloable content/ additional you have aquired for the game. Not very sustainable if you ask me. This is what would be called lost media if no solution is available. In such cases as of the downloable content of "Heroes Might and Magic 4" you would be needed to modify/ alter the game so that the validation is bypassed. Hard procedures which are not consumer friendly. One of my favorite franchises by the developer Dice owned by Electronic Arts, Battlefield have 2 games that I now cannot access normally due to the way the games were designed on the service part. These were Battlefield 2 and Battlefield 2142. Now one have to install custom editions of the games in order to play them again. Not very sustainable if you ask me I must state yet again and may be unsafe also. I may come with several topics regarding media which are not being preserved. I wish for that in the future games and other media to be sustainable and not become lost media due to end of service from publishers. Alternatives should exist! I am happy that I can finally share my thoughts regarding this topic as a citizen of the EEA treaty. Thank you very much for your time!
